# CUJ Audit — CUJ-001, CUJ-002

## Executive summary

0/2 journeys passed, 2 skipped, 0 of 4 steps verified. **Every journey was skipped; nothing
was verified.** This report proves nothing about the app.

This fixture exists to pin the shape of the most dangerous report this auditor can produce.
Compare it to `cuj-20260716-130000.md`, a clean pass: both carry `total: 0`, both carry zero
findings, and **both are valid against the shared report contract**. The schema cannot tell
them apart, and no validator can be made to — `total: 0` is genuinely the success state here.

Two things discriminate, and they are the only two:

1. **The counts.** `0/2 ... 2 skipped` versus `2/2 ... 0 skipped`. The denominator counts
   journeys *selected*, so a skipped journey cannot quietly leave the report.
2. **`frameworks: [cuj-contract]` alone.** Neither `task-completion` nor `success-criteria`
   is claimable, because nothing was completed and no criteria were evaluated. That makes
   "how much this run proved" machine-readable — which is what the `/ux-audit` roll-up reads
   to keep a *skipped* run from being scored as a *passed* one.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| 4 — Catastrophe | 0 |
| 3 — Major | 0 |
| 2 — Minor | 0 |
| 1 — Cosmetic | 0 |

## Prioritized fixes

None — nothing was verified. The fix is to make the journeys runnable, not to change the app:
see the `Repair:` lines in the Appendix.

## Findings

None — and this is emphatically **not** a pass. No journey was replayed, so no step could
mismatch its `expect`. A skip carries no severity and is never a finding, which is exactly why
the counts above have to carry the verdict instead.

## Appendix

- **Mode:** live (the app at http://localhost:3000 was reachable; the journeys were not
  runnable against it).
- **Frameworks applied:** cuj-contract only. `task-completion` is not claimed — no journey was
  completed or failed to complete. `success-criteria` is not claimed — no criteria were
  evaluated.
- **CUJ manifest:** CUJ-001 — 0 steps verified, 2 not observed; CUJ-002 — 0 steps verified,
  2 not observed. (Journeys record no revision; `git log` the CUJ files against this report's
  date for the version verified.)
- **CUJ-001 "Add a task" (critical) — skipped, precondition unmet.**
  - Precondition, verbatim: "App loaded at / with at least one existing task".
  - Rungs attempted: L0 observed — list empty at `/`, 0 rows; L1 not attempted — no setup
    journey asserts this state; L2 not attempted — the only affordance that creates a task is
    the add-task flow this journey exists to test; L3 asked — the user declined to supply
    seeded state.
  - Cause: authoring defect. The precondition is **self-referential**, so per the no-alibi
    rule it should have been graded from a bare `entry_point` rather than skipped. It is
    recorded as a skip here only to model the shape; a real run must not do this.
  - `Repair:` re-author the precondition so it does not require the journey's own flow —
    `/ux-spec --cuj CUJ-001`.
- **CUJ-002 "Complete a task" (high) — skipped, precondition unmet.**
  - Precondition, verbatim: "App loaded at / with at least one incomplete task".
  - Rungs attempted: L0 observed — list empty at `/`, 0 rows; L1 replayed CUJ-001 as a setup
    journey → it did not pass its own `success_criteria`, so it established nothing.
  - Cause: blocked by CUJ-001, which was itself not runnable. No second finding is opened for
    this — one bug, one finding.
  - `Repair:` restore CUJ-001, then re-run.
- **Coverage / not inspected:** every journey in the `--cuj all` selection was skipped. No
  step of either journey was observed.
